text.title: Launch Thread — Core Rules
text.summary: Five testable rules for the 7-tweet indie-hacker launch thread structure.

rule.statement: Thread has EXACTLY 7 tweets in this order: (1) HOOK + product name + 1-line value, (2) DEMO gif/video, (3) PROBLEM the product solves, (4) SOLVE — how product solves it, (5) PRICE + tiers, (6) CTA — single landing URL, (7) SOCIAL-PROOF — early-user quote or metric. More than 7 → engagement drops 30%+ per added tweet (Typefully cohort).

rationale.source: Typefully thread-length analysis 2023 + Marc Lou launch playbook

rule.rationale: 7 tweets is the empirical sweet spot for indie launches. The first 3 tweets must hook; the last 3 must convert. The 7-tweet shape allocates these zones.

rule.statement: Tweet 1 ≤ 240 chars (preview margin for retweets). Tweet 2 has < 80 chars + media. Tweets 3-5 ≤ 280 chars. Tweet 6 (CTA) ≤ 180 chars + URL. Tweet 7 ≤ 240 chars + screenshot. Twitter's algorithm clips longer tweets in feed previews.
rule.rationale: Tweets above ~240 chars in feed get truncated; readers click "show more" 30% less often. Tweet 1's preview margin is the highest-leverage variable in thread reach.

rule.statement: Tweet 2 MUST attach a 6-15s demo gif or short video. Tweet 7 MUST attach a screenshot (user testimonial, dashboard, metric). No media → thread engagement halves (Typefully 2023 data).
rule.rationale: Threads with media outperform text-only by 2-3× in impressions and 5-7× in clickthrough on the CTA tweet. The two specific media slots maximise the lift.

rule.statement: The thread contains EXACTLY ONE clickable URL — in tweet 6 — pointing at the launch landing page. No URLs in tweet 1 (hurts reach), no second CTA in tweet 7. The CTA tweet may include "DM me" as a secondary, but no second link.
rule.rationale: External URLs depress reach. Concentrating one link in tweet 6 captures click-through after the hook+demo+price are already in the reader's head. Multiple links dilute clicks AND reach.

rule.statement: Tweet 7's social proof MUST be a verbatim quote from a real user (with consent) OR a measurable metric ("$2,400 MRR in 14 days"). Generic praise ("loved by founders") is forbidden — readers detect it instantly.

rationale.source: Wynter B2B copy research / FTC endorsement guides

rule.rationale: Generic social proof reduces trust. Verbatim quote + name + role + a number is the only social-proof shape that lifts conversion at launch.

example.title: Launch thread skeleton (with placeholders)
example.note: Every tweet has a single purpose. Hook on top; conversion at bottom.

example.title: Multi-CTA thread (r4 violation)
example.code:
Tweet 1: "Launching today! Try it at example.com" (URL too early) Tweet 3: "Also follow me at @op and join my Discord at ..." (3 CTAs) Tweet 6: "DM me OR sign up at example.com/signup OR start trial at example.com/trial OR ..." (multi-link)
Result: clickthrough dilutes 3-5×; algorithm depresses reach; audience confused about the primary action.
example.note: Violates r4. Single CTA is non-negotiable for launches.
